generated_oml:
// C2: Physical Acting Components
instance HeatBedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "A controlled heating element that raises the temperature of incubator contents for precise thermal regulation"
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical actuator distributing heated air uniformly across the incubator space"
]

instance Controller : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Central unit executing bang-bang control commands for heatbed/fan based on DT predictions and safety protocols"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance IncubatorTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer (single-channel) measuring temperature at specific incubator location"
    DTDFVocab:producedData IncubatorTemperatureDataStream
]

instance IncubatorTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer (single-channel) measuring temperature at another strategic incubator location"
    DTDFVocab:producedData IncubatorTemperatureDataStream
]

instance IncubatorTemperatureSensor3 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "DS18S20 High-Precision 1-Wire Digital Thermometer (single-channel) measuring temperature at third strategic incubator location"
    DTDFVocab:producedData IncubatorTemperatureDataStream
]

// C4: Data Transmission
instance IncubatorTemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IncubatorTemperatureSensor1, IncubatorTemperatureSensor2, IncubatorTemperatureSensor3
    DTDFVocab:protocol OneWireBus
    DTDFVocab:accuracy ±0.5°C
    DTDFVocab:range -55°C to +125°C
    DTDFVocab:samplingFrequency LowFrequency
]

// C10: Models/Data Components
instance SimplifiedThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simplified thermal model with two free parameters (heat capacity and thermal inertia of incubator contents)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo NonLinearLeastSquaresSolver
]

instance DetailedThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Detailed thermal model with four free parameters capturing additional thermal behaviors"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo NonLinearLeastSquaresSolver
]

instance TemperatureSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw temperature readings from DS18S20 sensors in -55°C to +125°C range with ±0.5°C accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:fromData IncubatorTemperatureDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo NonLinearLeastSquaresSolver
]

instance CalibrationParameters : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Optimized parameter estimates from non-linear least squares solver minimizing objective function J"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureSensorData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimplifiedThermalModel, DetailedThermalModel
]

// C11: Enablers
instance NonLinearLeastSquaresSolver : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python-based solver implementing non-linear least squares optimization for model calibration"
    DTDFVocab:enables DynamicBehaviorPredictionService, RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance RabbitMQMessageBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "RabbitMQ server facilitating asynchronous communication between sensors, calibration algorithms, and monitoring systems"
    DTDFVocab:enables DynamicBehaviorPredictionService, RealTimeMonitoringService, OptimizationAndControlService
]

instance RaspberryPiEdgeComputer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Raspberry Pi device performing edge computing for real-time data processing and model inference"
    DTDFVocab:enables DynamicBehaviorPredictionService, RealTimeMonitoringService, OptimizationAndControlService
]

instance PythonSciPyLibrary : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python libraries (SciPy) for mathematical modeling and optimization tasks"
    DTDFVocab:enables NonLinearLeastSquaresSolver
]

// C6: Core Services
instance DynamicBehaviorPredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service reproducing and predicting incubator's thermal behavior under current/future operating conditions"
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureForecastInsight, StateTrajectoryInsight, FaultDetectionInsight
    DTDFVocab:enables SimplifiedThermalModel, DetailedThermalModel
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service processing sensor data to update DT models and detect deviations from expected behavior"
    DTDFVocab:provides CalibrationStatusInsight, SafetyViolationAlert, PerformanceDegradationAlert
    DTDFVocab:enables NonLinearLeastSquaresSolver, RabbitMQMessageBroker, RaspberryPiEdgeComputer
]

instance OptimizationAndControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling closed-loop optimization for actuator settings and system efficiency"
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterAdjustmentAction, AdaptiveControlAction
    DTDFVocab:enables NonLinearLeastSquaresSolver, RabbitMQMessageBroker, RaspberryPiEdgeComputer
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing interactive visualizations bridging cyber-physical domains"
    DTDFVocab:provides DashboardVisualization
    DTDFVocab:enables RaspberryPiEdgeComputer
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance TemperatureForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive time-series forecast of incubator temperature dynamics under current operating conditions"
]

instance StateTrajectoryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted temperature state trajectories showing expected system evolution over time"
]

instance FaultDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Anomaly analysis identifying potential faults (sensor failures, mechanical wear) via deviation detection"
]

instance CalibrationStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time status of model calibration accuracy and parameter estimation quality"
]

instance SafetyViolationAlert : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Alert triggered when temperature exceeds safety limits or safety protocols are violated"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PerformanceDegradationAlert : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Alert indicating system performance degradation requiring maintenance intervention"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ParameterAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of actuator settings (heatbed/fan) based on optimization algorithms"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AdaptiveControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of control parameters to adapt to operational changes (load shifts, maintenance)"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DashboardVisualization : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboard showing real-time temperature data, model predictions, and alerts"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ModelParameterUpdate : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time update of model parameters using non-linear least squares solver with new sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
    DTDFVocab:enables NonLinearLeastSquaresSolver
]

// C1: Physical Twin Environment
instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains HeatBedActuator, FanActuator, Controller,
                 IncubatorTemperatureSensor1, IncubatorTemperatureSensor2, IncubatorTemperatureSensor3
]

instance ThermalIncubatorSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Research-grade thermal incubator with insulated box, heatbed, and fan for precise temperature control"
    base:isContainedIn IncubatorEnvironment
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment IncubatorEnvironment
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system synchronizes data collection and model updates through **periodic data reading** from the Physical Twin (PT) via a low-level driver, ensuring temporal alignment between physical and digital components. The system employs **calibration algorithms** to iteratively refine models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment based on real-time sensor data. While the exact sampling frequency is not specified, the provided context implies a structured, time-driven workflow where model parameters (e.g., **Heating duration 'H'** and **Waiting duration 'C'**) are systematically updated to maintain consistency between the PT and DT. No asynchronous event-driven synchronization mechanisms are mentioned; the process relies on deterministic, periodic synchronization for accurate twinning behavior."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates three core components: **Data** (collected from the PT via sensors over time), **Models** (representing both cyber and physical aspects of the PT and its environment), and **Algorithms** (including fault detectors, supervisory controllers, state estimators, and optimizers). The architecture is centralized, where calibration algorithms update models and data streams to inform monitors for safety and optimality checks. While decentralized coordination mechanisms are not explicitly mentioned, the system implies a hierarchical structure where human or algorithmic interventions occur at the PT level upon detected violations. The scope of responsibilities is implicitly tied to the DT’s role in enabling real-time decision-making and predictive analysis for the PT’s operational performance."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
  A Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle encompasses phases where models evolve from abstract representations to operational integration with the Physical Twin (PT). The lifecycle stages include:

  **1. Conceptualization & Design Phase**:
  - Models are developed as abstract representations focusing on specific aspects (e.g., CAD models storing geometric properties like wheel sizes, chassis shapes, or styling attributes).
  - Dynamic models (e.g., differential equations) are created to analyze physical behaviors (e.g., forces affecting car movement) for optimization, cause-effect analysis, or scenario prediction.

  **2. Development & Calibration Phase**:
  - Models are refined using calibration algorithms to align cyber-physical representations with real-world data from sensors on the PT.
  - The DT integrates **data** (time-series sensor inputs), **models** (cyber-physical knowledge), and **algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators) to generate insights.

  **3. Deployment & Operation Phase**:
  - DTs are deployed alongside the PT, enabling real-time monitoring, predictive analytics, and decision support via continuous data-model-algorithm loops.
  - Calibration algorithms dynamically update models based on operational data to maintain accuracy under changing conditions.

  **Lifecycle Governance**:
  - Models are retained post-deployment to support continuous improvement, optimization, and fault detection.
  - Transition management involves iterative updates (e.g., recalibration) to ensure alignment between DT and PT across all phases.
  - No explicit lifecycle governance frameworks (e.g., ISO standards) are mentioned, but the emphasis is on iterative integration and model fidelity.
  "
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
**Model Fidelity**:
- **Requirements**: The DT relies on **two distinct models** (Model A/B) with varying parameter complexities, ensuring fidelity to both simplified and detailed thermal behaviors.
- **Validation/Verification**:
  - **Method**: A **non-linear least squares solver** is used to calibrate model parameters against a single data trajectory (sensor readings), minimizing the objective function **J** to achieve minimal prediction error.
  - **Uncertainty Quantification**: Not explicitly quantified in the document, but implied to rely on calibration error bounds derived from the solver’s convergence metrics.
- **Error Detection/Correction**:
  - **Mechanisms**: The DT employs **calibration algorithms** to iteratively update model parameters using real-time sensor data, ensuring alignment between cyber and physical twins.
  - **Quality Assurance**: Safety protocols enforce **bang-bang control** for actuators, preventing unsafe deviations while allowing adaptive adjustments via the DT.
- **Uncertainty Sources**: Primarily from sensor noise (±0.5°C) and model parameter approximations, mitigated through continuous calibration.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin employs **RabbitMQ** as the communication server for data exchange between the physical and virtual components. Connections are facilitated via **Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and GPIO** interfaces, as implied by the context of sensor integration. No explicit protocols like Ethernet/IP or PROFINET are mentioned, nor are bandwidth/latency specifications provided. The network topology is not detailed, but the system relies on a local setup (e.g., Raspberry Pi 4 Model B) for hosting. Security protocols are not referenced, and redundancy mechanisms are not specified beyond the general framework of calibration and monitoring algorithms."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin is deployed on a **local infrastructure** comprising a **Raspberry Pi 4 Model B** as the hosting platform. The system integrates a **RabbitMQ server** for communication, with code stored in an **online repository** for versioning and deployment. Computational and storage requirements are implied by the need for real-time calibration and monitoring but are not quantified. Scalability mechanisms are not detailed, and deployment models (e.g., cloud/edge/on-premise) are not explicitly categorized beyond the local hardware setup. Availability/reliability assurance is implied through the integration of calibration algorithms and monitoring systems, but no specific metrics (e.g., uptime guarantees) are provided."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "A Digital Twin (DT) system in the provided context emphasizes **safety-critical system considerations** through an integrated framework combining **monitoring, calibration algorithms, and fail-safe mechanisms**. The DT employs **calibration algorithms** to dynamically update cyber-physical system (CPS) and environmental models with real-time sensor data to ensure accurate parameter estimation. This process enables **real-time monitoring** of system performance, where deviations from optimal or safe operating conditions trigger automated or human-driven interventions via supervisory controllers or state estimators. The system inherently incorporates **risk assessment approaches** by leveraging data-driven models to predict faults or anomalies, ensuring compliance with safety protocols. While the documents do not specify explicit cybersecurity protocols, authentication systems, or access control mechanisms, the core safety framework relies on **algorithm-driven validation** of model-data consistency to mitigate risks associated with physical twin (PT) deviations."
]
